assertions.description: ::selection is recognized as a highlight pseudo-element selector.
::spelling-error is recognized as a highlight pseudo-element selector.
::grammar-error is recognized as a highlight pseudo-element selector.
Named custom highlights parse with the ::highlight(name) selector syntax.
A descendant ::selection using inherit receives color and background-color from its parent's ::selection highlight.
assertions.test: (() => { const style = document.createElement('style'); style.textContent = '#hl-parent::selection{color:rgb(1, 2, 3);background-color:rgb(4, 5, 6)} #hl-parent #hl-child::selection{color:inherit;background-color:inherit}'; const parent = document.createElement('p'); parent.id = 'hl-parent'; const child = document.createElement('em'); child.id = 'hl-child'; child.textContent = 'child'; parent.append('parent ', child); document.body.append(style, parent); const selection = getComputedStyle(child, '::selection'); const ok = selection.color === 'rgb(1, 2, 3)' && selection.backgroundColor === 'rgb(4, 5, 6)'; parent.remove(); style.remove(); return ok; })()

assertions.description: A descendant ::selection can override color while inheriting an unspecified background-color from the parent highlight.
assertions.test: (() => { const style = document.createElement('style'); style.textContent = '#hl-example::selection{color:rgb(10, 20, 30);background-color:rgb(40, 50, 60)} #hl-example > em::selection{color:rgb(70, 80, 90)}'; const parent = document.createElement('p'); parent.id = 'hl-example'; const child = document.createElement('em'); child.textContent = 'child'; parent.append('parent ', child); document.body.append(style, parent); const selection = getComputedStyle(child, '::selection'); const ok = selection.color === 'rgb(70, 80, 90)' && selection.backgroundColor === 'rgb(40, 50, 60)'; parent.remove(); style.remove(); return ok; })()

assertions.description: :root::selection can provide a document-wide selection theme that descendants inherit through the highlight chain.
assertions.test: (() => { const style = document.createElement('style'); style.textContent = ':root{--hl-bg:rgb(11, 22, 33);--hl-fg:rgb(44, 55, 66)} :root::selection{color:var(--hl-fg);background-color:var(--hl-bg)} #hl-root-child::selection{color:inherit;background-color:inherit}'; const child = document.createElement('strong'); child.id = 'hl-root-child'; child.textContent = 'child'; document.body.append(style, child); const selection = getComputedStyle(child, '::selection'); const ok = selection.color === 'rgb(44, 55, 66)' && selection.backgroundColor === 'rgb(11, 22, 33)'; child.remove(); style.remove(); return ok; })()

assertions.description: Custom properties used inside a highlight come from the originating element, preserving existing CSS-variable based selection themes.
assertions.test: (() => { const style = document.createElement('style'); style.textContent = '#hl-custom{--hl-bg:rgb(12, 34, 56)} #hl-custom::selection{background-color:var(--hl-bg)}'; const el = document.createElement('p'); el.id = 'hl-custom'; el.textContent = 'custom property'; document.body.append(style, el); const ok = getComputedStyle(el, '::selection').backgroundColor === 'rgb(12, 34, 56)'; el.remove(); style.remove(); return ok; })()
